title: gymnanthemum subcrassulescens
intro: Gymnanthemum subcrassulescens is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as a type of sage or salvia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Consuming the leaves of Gymnanthemum subcrassulescens has been associated with several health benefits, including antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-cancer properties, which may help to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, can be used to support respiratory health and may help to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
